title: Jürgen Habermas Showed What Philosophy Could Be
summary: After more than seventy years writing and thinking about democracy, capitalism, and the possibility of emancipatory politics, the German philosopher Jürgen Habermas died on Saturday, March 14, at age ninety-six. For a generation of political theorists and philosophers, his work was a touchstone. The author of over thirty books, he was interested in fundamental questions […]
reason: A philosophical obituary is primarily representational — a tribute to ideas rather than a demonstration of capture mechanisms or commune-layer activity in operation.

title: The Removed DOGE Deposition Videos Have Already Been Backed Up Across the Internet
summary: On Friday, a judge ordered those who uploaded the videos to YouTube to remove them. By Saturday, a backup of the videos was available online as a torrent and on the Internet Archive.
reason: This is a concrete example of routing architecture in action — an attempt to suppress information through legal/platform channels is immediately circumvented by distributed backup infrastructure outside those channels.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: the court order routes through YouTube (a centralized chokepoint), but the internet's distributed protocols — torrents, Internet Archive — provide alternative routing that the legal capture mechanism cannot reach.

title: Alien Life Might Exist on the Starless Moons of Rogue Planets, Scientists Say
summary: Moons orbiting free-floating planets may remain warm for billions of years, raising the possibility some might host stable water, or even life.
reason: Speculative astrobiology with no connection to capture mechanisms, enclosure, or commons dynamics.
